question: A $8~\text{kg}$ object is dropped from a height of $28~\text{m}$. Ignoring air resistance, calculate its velocity just before it hits the ground. Use $g = 9.8~\text{m/s}^2$.
explanation: Using conservation of mechanical energy: $mgh = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \Rightarrow v = \sqrt{2gh} = \sqrt{2(9.8)(28)} \approx 23.43~\text{m/s}$.

question: A $7~\text{kg}$ object accelerates from $9~\text{m/s}$ to $19~\text{m/s}$. Calculate the net work done on the object.

explanation: Using the work-energy theorem: $W_{net} = \Delta E_k = \frac{1}{2}m(v_f^2 - v_i^2) = 0.5(12)(28^2 - 3^2) = 4650~\text{J}$.

question: A $10~\text{kg}$ object is dropped from a height of $18~\text{m}$. Ignoring air resistance, calculate its velocity just before it hits the ground. Use $g = 9.8~\text{m/s}^2$.
explanation: Using conservation of mechanical energy: $mgh = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 \Rightarrow v = \sqrt{2gh} = \sqrt{2(9.8)(18)} \approx 18.78~\text{m/s}$.
